body_txt: Bob is in a job that's making him more miserable by the day. Never mind that it's his (probably only) source of income; quite simply, the job sucks and is draining him of energy and patience. Maybe he works in a Dangerous Workplace . Maybe he's working for Incompetence, Inc. Very possibly, there's No Such Thing as H.R. ; or there is , but he sure wishes there wasn't . Perhaps he is worried about the security of his employment status and his overbearing supervisor or office manager does nothing to help. Maybe he just recently got Promoted to Scapegoat or has to put up with an incompetent boss. He could be the Beleaguered Assistant , or the Only Sane Employee in an office populated by those who should rightly be certified insane. Chances are good he's been subjected to the Delegation Relay one time too many. Or perhaps the boss is a Corrupt Corporate Executive and Bob's morals can't allow him to stick around that kind of environment. The reasons vary, but the result is the same. Resentment builds … Tranquil Fury is fostered … and then he reaches The Last Straw . "You know what? TAKE THIS JOB AND SHOVE IT! " The recipient may be a Jerkass who thoroughly deserved it . The employer/supervisor may also be a parent or relative or somebody who's SUPPOSED to be a good guy. Note that it doesn't have to be the word-for-word line of this trope's title that is said; the manner of Rage Quit displayed against the supervisor/boss is essentially the heart of the trope. Be careful, though; if done too often, it may lead to Why Do You Keep Changing Jobs? queries (in which case, it might be you who has the real problem). If the person goes on a violent rampage instead of simply quitting, that's Going Postal . If they have to return humbly, that's Crossing the Burnt Bridge . Even if you personally have never quit a job in anger, you probably know at least one person who has, making this trope very much Truth in Television . Compare Screw This, I'm Outta Here! (for a range of situations that may or may not involve one's job), Rage Quit (for angrily quitting a game rather than a job) and Insignia Rip-Off Ritual (for examples of quitting or being fired that are symbolized by destroying an icon related to the job). May involve a "The Reason You Suck" Speech . A subtrope is Resign in Protest , where a specific action or decision by their organization leaves someone feeling unable to continue doing the job in good conscience and leaving as a form of protest. If Bob is forbidden from leaving or is forced back in not of his own accord, he's subjected to Resignations Not Accepted or Mandatory Unretirement . Not related to To Hell with This Infernal Job , though the subject of that trope may very well be feeling this way when they quit their own job.
